auditScope: Driver load data availability, existing calculations, and V1 implementation feasibility

driverPay.notes: Driver-specific pay amount. Currently uses load.price as proxy. Can be added in Phase 2.

commissionPercent.source: N/A - not in loads table (exists on users table as global setting)
commissionPercent.notes: Commission rate for this load. Available on users.commissionPercent but not per-load. Can be added in Phase 2.

pickupMiles.notes: Distance from origin to pickup. Not currently calculated. Can be derived in Phase 2 with route planning.

deliveryMiles.notes: Distance from pickup to delivery. Currently merged into totalMiles via Haversine.

itemCount.notes: Number of items/packages. Not in schema. Defaults to 1 for time estimation in V1. Can be added in Phase 2.

stopCount.notes: Number of delivery stops. Not in schema. Defaults to 1 in V1. Can be added in Phase 2.

serviceType.notes: Type of service (delivery, pickup, both). Not in schema. Can be added in Phase 2.

estimatedDriveTime.notes: Drive time in minutes. Not in schema. Can be estimated from miles (120 miles ≈ 180 min at 40 mph) in V1.

estimatedShoppingTime.notes: Service/shopping time in minutes. Not in schema. Defaults to itemCount * 1.1 minutes in V1.

estimatedTotalTime.notes: Total time estimate. Can be derived from drive + service times in V1.

fuelType.notes: Vehicle fuel type (diesel, gasoline, electric). Not in schema. Defaults to cargo van in V1. Can be added in Phase 2.

fuelCostPerMile.notes: Operating cost per mile. Not in schema. Defaults to $0.179/mile (cargo van) in V1. Can be added in Phase 2.

vehicleCostPerMile.notes: Total vehicle cost per mile. Not in schema. Defaults to $0.179/mile in V1. Can be calculated from components in Phase 2.

estimatedFuel.notes: Estimated fuel cost for this load

netMargin.notes: Pre-calculated margin (price - fuel - tolls). Stored in DB.

netPay.notes: Basic net profit. Does NOT include vehicle operating costs. This is the 'profit' field returned by normalizeLoadFinancials().

vehicleCost.notes: NOT CALCULATED. Need to implement vehicle cost engine. Can use default $0.179/mile in V1.

netProfit.notes: Same as netPay. Does NOT include vehicle operating costs.

payPerMile.notes: Revenue per mile. Uses gross price, not net pay.

payPerHour.notes: NOT CALCULATED. Requires time estimates (missing from schema). Can be derived in V1 using safe defaults.

estimatedTimeMinutes.notes: NOT CALCULATED. Requires time data (missing from schema). Can be estimated in V1 using defaults.

driverCommission.notes: NOT CALCULATED. Commission field missing from loads table. Can fetch from users.commissionPercent in V1.

settlementAmount.notes: Settlement totals calculated from settlement loads

miles.formula: R = 3958.8 (earth radius in miles); dLat = (deliveryLat - pickupLat) * π/180; dLng = (deliveryLng - pickupLng) * π/180; a = sin²(dLat/2) + cos(pickupLat*π/180) * cos(deliveryLat*π/180) * sin²(dLng/2); c = 2 * atan2(√a, √(1-a)); miles = R * c * 1.15 (trucking adjustment)

margin.notes: Margin percentage. Based on gross price, not net pay.

missingFields.impact: V1 uses load.price as proxy. Phase 2 adds per-load driver pay.

missingFields.reason: Not in loads table (exists on users table as global setting)
missingFields.impact: V1 fetches from users.commissionPercent. Phase 2 adds per-load commission.
V1 defaults to 1. Phase 2 adds real item count for accurate time estimates.
V1 defaults to 1. Phase 2 adds real stop count.
V1 estimates from miles (120 miles ≈ 180 min at 40 mph). Phase 2 adds real time estimates.
V1 estimates from itemCount (default 1.1 min/item). Phase 2 adds real service time.
V1 defaults to cargo van. Phase 2 adds real fuel type.
V1 uses default $0.179/mile. Phase 2 adds vehicle-specific costs.

grossPay.notes: Use driverPay if available (Phase 2), otherwise use load.price

totalMiles.notes: Use Haversine-calculated miles from normalizeLoadFinancials(), fallback to 120

serviceTimeMinutes.notes: Default 1.1 minutes per item if itemCount missing

estimatedTotalMinutes.notes: Total time with buffer, minimum 15 minutes

vehicleCost.notes: Cargo van operating cost per mile

netPay.notes: Net pay after vehicle operating costs (does NOT include fuel/tolls already in load)

hourlyRate.notes: Estimated hourly rate based on net pay and time

positives: Core load data already available (price, fuel, tolls, coordinates)
Distance calculation already implemented (Haversine in normalizeLoadFinancials)
Basic profit calculation already exists (price - fuel - tolls)
No database schema changes required for V1
Can use safe defaults for missing data
All UI components can be frontend-only
Existing data enrichment pipeline (normalizeLoadFinancials) can be leveraged
concerns: Time estimates missing (can use defaults based on distance)
Vehicle cost not calculated (can use default $0.179/mile)
Commission field missing from loads (can fetch from user settings)
No per-load driver pay (can use load.price as proxy)
mitigation: Use safe defaults with clear 'Estimated' disclaimers
Plan Phase 2 for schema changes and real calculations
Implement defaults in frontend utility functions
Add admin settings for default vehicle costs by type

phase1.name: Frontend-Only V1
integration: Update DriverOps.tsx to use new components
Add detail drawer for full breakdown

phase2.name: Backend Enhancement
backendChanges: Add itemCount field to loads table
Add estimatedDriveTime field to loads table
Add estimatedShoppingTime field to loads table
Add driverPay field to loads table
Add commissionPercent field to loads table
calculations: Real time estimates from route data
Real vehicle costs from driver settings

auditConclusion.summary: The current system has sufficient data to implement a V1 driver load earnings UI with safe defaults. Core calculations (gross pay, net pay, distance, profitability) can be implemented immediately using existing data (price, fuel, tolls, coordinates) and safe defaults (vehicle cost $0.179/mile, time estimates 1.1 min/item). No database schema changes required for V1. All components can be frontend-only.
keyFindings: 8 fields missing (can use defaults in V1)
6 calculations can be derived from existing data
nextSteps: 2. Create DriverLoadEarningsCalculator utility with safe defaults
9. Plan Phase 2 for schema changes and real calculations
